rankings.idea: DiD-Transformer — Distributional Treatment Effects on Career Trajectories
rankings.rationale: Highly novel methodological contribution combining transformer representations with difference-in-differences. Strong feasibility: synthetic validation passes, real data pre-trained. Identification is creative — the four-adapter design with temporal masking is well-motivated. Risk: may be seen as more ML than economics by some referees.

rankings.idea: DiD-Transformer — Distributional Treatment Effects on Career Trajectories
rankings.rationale: The paper proposes a genuinely new identification strategy by leveraging transformer weight-space arithmetic for causal inference. Synthetic validation is thorough (8 DGPs). TVA application is well-chosen as a benchmark. Main concern: whether econometricians will accept the identification argument without formal asymptotic theory.

rankings.idea: DiD-Transformer — Distributional Treatment Effects on Career Trajectories
rankings.rationale: Outstanding novelty — this bridges two literatures (causal inference and representation learning) in a way that hasn't been done. The 10.85M individual linked panel is impressive data infrastructure. Key strength: the method recovers distributional objects that standard DiD fundamentally cannot. Key risk: bootstrap inference for the transformer-based estimator lacks theoretical grounding.
